review_title: Five Stars and a Thumbs Up!
description: I recommend Salesforce to anyone. There's also the knowledge base where I can find the information I need for the customers.
author: Kenidra S.
pros: What I like most about Salesforce is how the customer's information populates. I can see all their information.
cons: Sometimes the costumers profile don't populate so we have create a profile for them. This usually doesn't happen.

review_title: A here-to-stay CRM app requiring knowledgeable staff to fully appreciate ppreciate
description: I'd been using HubSpot for a while and had been eyeing Salesforce as an alternative. To be honest, I feel like they are quite on par, and as a small business owner you can't really go wrong with either. Salesforce CRM is definitely adequate. Especially the Marketing Cloud for managing email campaigns on customers, which makes things organised and easy to track at a glance and go through analytics. If you were managing things on your own like myself, Salesforce might be too sophisticated for its own good as a start, but something you can start looking into if you were looking to scale or to sell to various markets.
author: Athena M.
pros: It is a sophisticated CRM software, and great for managing customer database and workflows, but more feared towards larger enterprises I feel, and not exactly cost-effective for small business owners.
cons: The steep fee for various subscription tiers, the initial details up was lengthy to set up and I had to really wrap my head around it, felt that it is quite advanced.

review_title: A Powerful (Albeit Flawed) Tool for the Enterprise
description: Marketing Cloud is much more than an email management platform. It is capable of things that were way beyond the scope of my organization's needs. And it was just too expensive to justify renewing our contract when we only used a quarter of its functionality. Larger orgs and multi-brand enterprises are definitely the target market for this tool, not small nonprofits like my employer.
author: Taylor O.
pros: Salesforce Marketing Cloud is the most powerful marketing automation platform I've used in my ten year career. Its send speed is unmatched, even on multi-step automations across different channels.
cons: The user interface is pretty awful. It looks like five or six different tools smashed together with one unifying menu bar (because that is what it is). This makes it hard to learn and greatly hampers ease of use for non-technical employees. The syncing with Salesforce Sales Cloud also leaves a lot to be desired. It does a good job of ingesting CRM data, but doesn't round-trip any of the changes made within SFMC to the CRM (i.e. updated contact info).

description: I love sales force! To me it has completely eliminated excel spreadsheets. I now 100% keep up with my business in salesforce only!
author: Andrew W.
pros: As a sales agent sales force provides me a place to easily keep up with my book of business while providing me the tools to retain and grow my business!
cons: Sales force does great at reminders but I would love to see AI/AI assisted programs being integrated.

review_title: Easy to use, but not a great value
author: Britta B.
pros: It is fairly easy to use and create content in
cons: It is expensive, and they charge you ala carte for everything

review_title: Super expensive enterprise software but at least feels like abandoned by the dev team.
description: Literally everything felt ancient and abandoned. It was clunky like 2003 have just called and ask to return it back.
author: Matt K.
pros: Being able to set up simple automation rules.
cons: Engagement Programs. You weren't able to track replies, it didn't allow setting up an email that will be able to receive replies. The email creator was super old-fashioned comparing to way cheaper alternatives where you can easily build emails using drag-n-drop sections.

review_title: Marketing Cloud and Salesforce are a powerful combination.
description: We have had a very positive experience with Marketing Cloud. It is a powerful tool, especially when combined with Salesforce.
author: Jeffrey C.
pros: Marketing Cloud integrates well with Salesforce. We schedule and automate communications, deriving the audience from live Salesforce data.
cons: There is a significant learning curve with Marketing Cloud. The Marketing Cloud user interface seems disjointed.

description: Overall, Salesforce Marketing Cloud has been a mixed but workable experience — it delivers strong email management and template capabilities with a polished user interface, but the complexity, occasional performance hiccups, and variable customer support mean it takes time and commitment to get real value out of it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The platform's email management and template builder are robust, the user interface is generally well-organized, and its integration with the broader Salesforce ecosystem makes it a powerful choice for managing large-scale campaigns and customer data in one place.
cons: The steep learning curve makes ease of use a challenge for new users, performance and speed can lag during heavy campaigns, customer support response times are inconsistent, and the overall value for money feels high unless you're leveraging the full suite of features.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy to use and friendly customer service
description: My overall experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud is positive and well received by me and my peers.
author: Carli N.
pros: What I like most about Salesforce Marketing cloud is it's easy to use layout as well as fast and friendly customer service.
cons: I cannot think of anything at this time that I dislike about Salesforce Marketing Cloud. Sometimes they are doing system maintenance but that's standard.

description: Salesforce marketing cloud is one of the best way to maintain such as a strong relationships with customers and grow your business.
author: Rachid K.
pros: The system provide many ways to grow the business you have.
cons: Customisation of emails into customers helps to get a strong relationships reports.

description: Working with the support team is extremely painful. We spent hours over multiple weeks showing them our issues over video. Their premium support resources often couldn't figure out how to resolve our issues. It felt like they were just as fed up with this crap tool as we are. Although they didn't say it, I think one of our key employees quit over the frustration of dealing with marketing cloud.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I don't like anything about this software.
cons: The integration with Salesforce is WAY oversold in the marketing materials. Moving data back and forth between the two products is extremely finicky and prone to breaking. Expect lots of duplication of data and lists that will invariably be out of sync. Managing email opt out lists with two systems of record from the same company is a CAN-SPAM nightmare. The integration's administrative tools can't be accessed in lightning. The customer facing unsubscribe and profile management pages look like shit and can't be branded or customized. They are so old, they still provide customers a warning about using HTML emails with AOL. Your Salesforce users will see email sends and interactions in contact records for some but not all emails sent via marketing cloud. If you try to send to a campaign or report from Salesforce, email personalization won't work. Same thing for transactional emails. You will have to send every single piece of personalization data in the API call to marketing cloud, because MC won't be able to find it on its own, even though it has a list of all these attributes and is supposedly syncing with Salesforce. There is single sign on, but only by request, and provisioning of accounts is not supported. Journeys will break the minute you make the slightest change to them. Trying to split journeys based on Salesforce attributes often just doesn't work. Because it's Salesforce, anything you would actually want to do with this software comes at an additional cost.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I liked that this is integrated within the SFDC CRM and i do not have to use a separate platform for managing leads and opportunities with sending email marketing and outreach.
cons: i do not have anything i dislike so far, we constantly use this feature

review_title: Salesforce Marketing Cloud - The best in the game!
description: Salesforce Marketing Cloud gave us insights to customer trends, and the best market segmentation help we could ask for.
author: Iyer A.
pros: The high level of personalizable customer engagement, along with access to AI tools, and campaign management, and robust analytics as well. The customer service is great also.
cons: Salesforce marketing cloud randomly has extremely long slow periods, and any updates do take a bit of a while. Not much outside of that

description: I utilized this successfully aid in my Email marketing & advertising ,and I look forward to utilizing this tool in many other ways In the near future!
author: Kurt J.
pros: The way it is excellent for identifying leads, and hopefully acquaints these leads with services, etc..
cons: A bit too technical at times, but that could be on me @ my knowledge

author: Mark B.
pros: Great storage of data. Able to look at analytics and properly guage information.
cons: Doesn't have any automation. Have to take time to put in data

author: Miriam A.
pros: Easy integration with existing email lists
cons: The high cost and no automatic way of importing multiple emails per contact
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: IT IS GOOD BUT COULD BE IMPROVED
author: KARINA U.
pros: IT IS EASY AND GOOD , BUT SOMETIMES IT HAS ISSUES IN CHROME .
cons: IS NOT WORKING 100% GOOD WITH CHROME , BUT IT IS OK WITH SAFARI
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: JENMI G.
pros: very easy to navigate, if any issues it does not take long for help
cons: there is not much i do not like from this

review_title: I recommend salesforce marketing cloud to the costumer
author: Niel Winston T.
pros: Discount i like the discount that given to the costumer i really do like this site
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Campaign Management and Email Marketing Management with SFMC
description: Saleforce Marketing Cloud is a marketing automation leader in the market. One best thing about salesforce marketing cloud is that it has all features of automating all your marketing campaigns for Email, SMS, Direct mail and push notification.
author: Anil R.
pros: My overall experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud is really great, because it is user friendly and easy to use marketing automation platform available in the market and with the help of it I am able to create Email campaigns, SMS campaigns, Direct mail campaigns and push notification campaigns. We also have purchased its Email studio, SMS studio and Push studio license.
cons: I do not have any dislike for this platform.

description: I have noticed it is much easier to contact a long list of patients per day and speeds us the process by making the call data more organized.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I thought Salesforce to be very user friendly and easily navigable. The home page is very clear as to which tabs pertain to what. My favorite feature is being able to change the tab names into the specific criteria I need for example one of them is "Patients" and another is "Call Interactions," this way you can build your own easily navigable website.
cons: There are no features I disliked about Salesforce Experience Cloud

review_title: Everything broke all the time and it had a significant negative impact on revenue
description: Never have I been so frustrated and upset with a platform as I have with SFMC. I run the marketing department for a small e-commerce company and SFMC was the cause of so many headaches. We encountered problem after problem with them (many noted in the "cons" section of this review") and our account manager / customer support was very frequently unable to successfully troubleshoot the issue. My small team had to spend hundreds of hours troubleshooting significant, bottom-line impacting issues caused by the platform with little to no help from Salesforce. At one point, we couldn't send any emails out of the platform for a number of weeks (the platform showed a 0% delivered rate), which, as an e-commerce company, drastically impacted sales and reduced our revenue. We also can't set up basic journeys in the platform, like abandoned carts, browse abandonments, win-back campaigns, post-purchase campaigns, or birthday/anniversary campaigns, meaning our email marketing program relies solely on a stead push of one-off marketing messages. Unless you have a large IT team with plenty of bandwidth to implement Salesforce and continually troubleshoot it, look elsewhere. SFMC is not a fit product to be on the market
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Robust data features, but difficult to deploy without technical resource
cons: - Transactional emails randomly turned off for months - error on SFMCs part caused high SPAM rating on our account, so very few emails on our list were receiving messages - no conversion tracking - very little integration with company or Google analytics - implementation was a nightmare and was never completed - DMARC/DKIM/SPF never set up - UX/UI difficult to use - support was terrible and couldn't even help us set up an abandoned cart email

description: For the first time user this is very easy to understand no need of any special training only demo video is enough for any user to start working on it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Experience and user friendly easy to login and easy to install.
cons: lease like about is like we have to take login every day that to with OTP authentication.

author: Ipek Nil B.
pros: Single most amazing CRM system available for email campaigns.
cons: This tool unfortunately comes with a huge cost, not only to buy but also to maintain.

description: Salesforce Marketing Cloud is very helpful software!
author: Ambar Y.
pros: - It is an excellent piece of software for social media marketing, lead generating, email management, and efficient email generation.- It's convenient because it's a one-stop shop. I have access to all I require to succeed in my current environment.
cons: There is no negative feedback or downside for this software. It helped us a lot in many things like content management, email marketing, social media marketing, etc. Excellent Tool!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
